notes: Very comprehensive at 385 lines; covers more products and criteria

All assertions pass 100% in both configurations — assertions are non-discriminating. They test for presence of content that any competent answer includes.
Real differentiation is in quality dimensions not captured: source citations, benchmark data, real-world case studies, current ecosystem awareness (2025-2026 data).
with_skill outputs consistently include sourced URLs and dated references; without_skill outputs do not.
with_skill is slower (147s avg vs 97s) and uses 2.25x more tokens — overhead from spawning and coordinating multiple haiku agents.
Eval 2 (RSC) is notable: without_skill produced a longer (344 vs 178 lines) and arguably more thorough answer covering 10 gotchas vs 6. Multi-agent decomposition didn't win on raw completeness here.
Eval 3 (vector DBs) is where with_skill most clearly adds value: Q1 2026 benchmark table, market trend analysis, ScyllaDB GA coverage.
Recommendation: strengthen assertions to test for sourced URLs, specific named tool/product counts, and benchmark/date presence to better discriminate between runs.
